summary: Large fighter CPU-attack decompilation PR for src/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.c. It replaced placeholders for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, and ftCo_800B77E8 with C, added typed local structs for attack-entry and x50-like data, adjusted ftCo_800B5AB0 call/prototype arity, changed Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 from int to u32, and made smaller matching-oriented edits in ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220. The only textual feedback is the decomp bot report showing partial score improvements, not full matches.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation work: added math_ppc include, typed ftCo_AttackEntry fields, added local ftCo_x50_attr and ftCo_x50_t structs, implemented several ftCo CPU attack functions, and made smaller matching-oriented edits in existing functions.
Header signature update for ftCo_800B5AB0 from two parameters to three parameters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftcpuattack.h hunk -/* 0B5AB0 */ int ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*); +/* 0B5AB0 */ int ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*, void*);

key_files.role: Type surface update: Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 changed from int to u32.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:types.h hunk -/* +50 */ int x50; +/* +50 */ u32 x50;

what_changed.text: Implemented previously undecompiled ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, and ftCo_800B5AB0 with attack-list filtering, predicted position/range checks, random weighted selection, and assert fallback paths.
Expanded ftCo_AttackEntry from mostly padding into address-style fields x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x1C, and x20 while retaining the known cmd and weight fields.
Added local temporary structs ftCo_x50_attr and ftCo_x50_t to express accesses in ftCo_800B5AB0 instead of leaving the second argument completely opaque.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftCo_800B5AB0 uses ftCo_x50_t* x50 and ftCo_x50_attr* attrs

what_changed.text: Implemented ftCo_800B77E8 as a fighter-kind switch that adds command IDs to cpu->xCC_array based on line-of-sight/platform checks, ground/air state, and character-specific constants.
Updated ftCo_800B8A9C to pass cpu->x50 as the new second argument to ftCo_800B5AB0, shifting the previous table argument to third position.
Made localized edits in ftCo_800BB220: initialized temp_r31 at declaration, changed an item-kind if/else into a switch, and introduced dx/dy/dz locals for repeated hit vector expressions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftCo_800BB220 hunks around temp_r31, switch (ip->kind), and dx/dy/dz locals

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded partial improvements for main/melee/ft/ftcpuattack, including ftCo_800B77E8 to 88.33%, ftCo_800B4AB0 to 91.73%, ftCo_800B52AC to 91.12%, ftCo_800B5AB0 to 93.60%, ftCo_800BB220 to 93.90%, .sdata2 to 91.88%, and ftCo_800B8A9C to 87.29%. These are score improvements, not evidence of full matching.

smart_moves.text: When a recovered function needed a real extra argument, the PR updated both the call site and the owning header prototype instead of hiding the mismatch with a local declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftCo_800B8A9C call to ftCo_800B5AB0 now has three args
raw_diff:ftcpuattack.h prototype now int ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*, void*)

smart_moves.text: The PR used small temporary structs for unknown x50 data, allowing field-style accesses at known offsets while preserving uncertainty with address-style field names.
For ftCo_800BB220, introducing dx/dy/dz locals narrowed repeated arithmetic without changing the surrounding collision-check structure.

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a function body proves an extra parameter is real, update the owning header and affected call sites together.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftCo_800B8A9C call changed to ftCo_800B5AB0(fp, (void*) cpu->x50, ((void**) Fighter_804D64FC->x14)[fp->kind])
raw_diff:ftcpuattack.h prototype changed to ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*, void*)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Temporary local structs with address-style field names are useful bridge code when an opaque argument has enough recovered offsets to express accesses as typed fields, but not enough evidence for semantic names.
Partial score-report improvements should be recorded as progress, not as proof of matched functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev[bot] report lists percentages below 100% for touched functions

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven globals/functions retained address-style names such as ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, and ftCo_800B77E8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:function definitions retain address-style ftCo_ names

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered but semantically unclear struct fields kept offset-style names such as x04, x08, xC1C, xC20, xC24, and xC28.
Clear local roles received short names such as fp, target, cpu, list, sel, count, and attrs; less-clear locals such as temp_r30 and var_r0 were not fully semanticized.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added <math_ppc.h> while using sqrtf in newly implemented prediction code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: PR evidence does not state why this include was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit f64 casts around gravity/prediction formulas in the new CPU attack selection functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:fpPredY and relPredY assignments with (f64) casts
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review or objdiff excerpt confirms which casts were required for matching.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed some literals from f32-suffixed to double literals in ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800B77E8-style calls, e.g. 5.0 and 1000.0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Only score-report evidence is available; do not generalize as a style rule without objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Converted an item-kind conditional in ftCo_800BB220 into a switch over ip->kind.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review comment explains the motivation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced dx/dy/dz locals for repeated hit-position deltas in ftCo_800BB220.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:three ftCo_800BB220 hunks adding f32 dx, dy, dz
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Treat as a local matching tactic; no global rule is supported.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Reported 7 improvements in unmatched items for GALE01 between 66afaf4 and 9699c9b, with all listed touched functions still below 100%.

follow_up_queries: ftCo_800BB220 dx dy dz switch ip->kind matching
ftCo_AttackEntry x04 x08 x0C x10 x14 weight x1C x20

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The loaded raw diff excerpt was truncated, but targeted local reads recovered the header/type hunks and later ftcpuattack.c hunks needed for intake.
Bot report provides score improvements only; it does not prove full matches, author intent, or reviewer acceptance of specific tactics.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but no review discussion is available to infer why individual code shapes were chosen.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header and call sites should move with recovered signature changes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2526 changed ftCo_800B5AB0 from two arguments to three and updated both the call in ftCo_800B8A9C and the declaration in ftcpuattack.h. This supports the existing truthful-headers-and-includes standard for recovered decomp signatures.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Temporary typed structs can bridge opaque CPU data
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2526 added local ftCo_x50_t and ftCo_x50_attr structs to replace fully opaque access in ftCo_800B5AB0 while keeping field names offset-based where semantics were not proven.

source_update_candidates.title: ftcpuattack.c CPU attack selection decomp progress in PR 2526
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.c was the primary file in PR 2526, with large implementations for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, and ftCo_800B77E8 plus smaller edits to ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220. Bot report showed partial unmatched-item improvements for this unit.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful searchable path context for future work, but should be curated as a path fact rather than promoted from intake.

source_update_candidates.title: Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 type changed in PR 2526
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2526 changed struct Fighter_x1A88_t field x50 from int to u32 in src/melee/ft/types.h and used it as an argument to ftCo_800B5AB0 from ftCo_800B8A9C.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful field-history fact; intake evidence supports the edit but not broader semantics.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the large repeated ftCo_800B77E8 switch blocks as an accepted source style; no review evidence explains whether a loop/table form was attempted.
Do not mark any function as fully matched from this slice; bot report percentages remain below 100%.
Do not infer semantic names for x50, x04, x08, xC1C, or similar offset-style fields from this PR alone.
Do not generalize double-literal or f64-cast use as a global matching rule without objdiff evidence.
